revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = {{ill|Comparison of area|ja|面積の比較|0.102}} | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of {{ill|Mikuni, Fukui|ja|三国町}} in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[ja:雄島]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (www.pref.fukui.lg.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|福井県の島|[[京都府]][[舞鶴市]]にある島|冠島|秋田県にある島|雄島 (秋田県)|宮城県にある島|松島#文芸}} {{Infobox 島 |島名=雄島 |画像=[[ファイル:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
上空から見た雄島 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |面積=[[面積の比較|0.102]] |周囲=2 |標高=27 |最高峰= |最大都市= |海域=[[日本海]] |国={{JPN}}（[[福井県]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=雄島}} |OSMズーム = 13 }} '''雄島'''（おしま）は[[福井県]][[坂井市]][[三国町]]安島にある[[島]]（無人島）。[[越前海岸]]にある島のひとつで、同海岸中もっとも大きな島である。[[越前加賀海岸国定公園]]に含まれている[https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html 福井県 越前加賀海岸国定公園]。 == 概要 == 面積は約10.2[[ヘクタール]]で、周囲2キロメートルは海食崖になっている{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=「雄島」の地質|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。島全体が[[流紋岩]]でできており、近隣の[[東尋坊]]同様に[[柱状節理]]が発達しているほか、板状節理も見ることができる{{Cite web|和書|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) 雄島の流紋岩（坂井市三国町）|work=福井のニュース ジオっちゃお|publisher=福井新聞|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=ふくいミュージアムNo.19|accessdate=2022-4-20|publisher=福井県立歴史博物館|page=6}}。[[スダジイ]]、[[タブノキ]]、[[シロダモ]]のように温暖な気候を好む植物が生えている{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=「雄島」の植生|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。 島の西側斜面上に[[雄島灯台]]が建てられている。 == 名所 == ; 磁石岩 : 何らかの理由で熱残留磁気が特に強く、近くに置いた[[方位磁針]]の向きがずれるほどの岩が何か所かある。[[雷]]に打たれたせいで磁界ができたとも言われる。 ; 瓜割の水 : 海岸に近い場所に湧く小さな[[泉]]だが、塩気のない真水である。雄島に降った[[雨]]が地中の節理に沿って流れ出た[[地下水]]で、夏でも冷たく、泉に入れた[[ウリ|瓜]]が割れたとされる。 ; 大湊神社 : [[白雉]]年間に勤請されたと伝えられる[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=「大湊神社」の由緒|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。[[源義経]]が落ち延びる際に立ち寄って家臣の[[兜]]を奉納した、社が[[朝倉義景]]の後援を受けていた際に[[明智光秀]]が訪れて[[漢詩]]を詠んだ{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=「雄島」の源（明智）光秀 詩碑|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}という話もある。 : かつての社殿は[[織田信長]]の兵によって焼き払われており、[[21世紀]]に残る社は棟札によると[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）に造立されたものである{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/03.html|title=「大湊神社」の社殿|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。本殿・拝殿ともに福井県の文化財に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|date=|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=坂井市|work=福井の文化財|publisher=福井県教育庁生涯学習・文化財課|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。 == ギャラリー == 画像:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|[[東尋坊]]から望む雄島。 File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|磁石岩。先端部の数か所で方位磁針が異常を示す。 File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|瓜割の水 File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|大湊神社 拝殿。この真後ろに本殿がある。 File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|雄島へ渡る雄島橋 File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|雄島から東尋坊を望む File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|大湊神社参道の石段 File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|三国の豪商内田惣右衛門が寄進した石灯篭 File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|タブノキの巨木 File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|ヤブラン File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|キツネノカミソリ File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|クズ File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|センニンソウ == 交通アクセス == * [[北陸新幹線]]・[[ハピラインふくい線]] [[芦原温泉駅]]から [[京福バス]] 85・97系統（東尋坊線）「[[坂井市龍翔博物館|龍翔博物館]]前」行きで「安島（あんとう）」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は[[えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線]] [[あわら湯のまち駅]]からも利用できる。 * えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線 [[三国駅 (福井県)|三国駅]]から 京福バス 84・96系統（東尋坊線）「芦原温泉駅」行きで「安島」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は同線の[[三国港駅]]からも利用できる。 島へは安島漁港から雄島橋が架かっており、徒歩または[[自転車]]でアクセス可能。橋の本土側そばに[[駐車場]]がある。駐車場までは{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[北陸自動車道]] [[金津インターチェンジ|金津IC]]から、主に広域農道「坂井丘陵フルーツライン」経由、約17 km。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ 雄島]（福井県坂井市公式観光ガイド さかい旅ナビ） * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしま}} [[Category:福井県の島]] [[Category:坂井市の地理]]

This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[ja:雄島]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōshima]][[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōshima]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = {{ill|Comparison of area|ja|面積の比較|0.102}} | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of {{ill|Mikuni, Fukui|ja|三国町}} in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] }} [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}

It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|12=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|12=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|12=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|12=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|12=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|12=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|12=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|12=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] }} [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}

It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|12=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|12=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|12=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|12=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|12=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|12=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}’s troops. {{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = {{ill|Comparison of area|ja|面積の比較|0.102|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of {{ill|Mikuni, Fukui|ja|三国町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}}

revision.comment: 59 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:雄島 up to rev 104322008
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|福井県の島|[[京都府]][[舞鶴市]]にある島|冠島|秋田県にある島|雄島 (秋田県)|宮城県にある島|松島#文芸}} {{Infobox 島 |島名=雄島 |画像=[[ファイル:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
上空から見た雄島 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |面積=[[面積の比較|0.102]] |周囲=2 |標高=27 |最高峰= |最大都市= |海域=[[日本海]] |国={{JPN}}（[[福井県]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=雄島}} |OSMズーム = 13 }} '''雄島'''（おしま）は[[福井県]][[坂井市]][[三国町]]安島にある[[島]]（無人島）。[[越前海岸]]にある島のひとつで、同海岸中もっとも大きな島である。[[越前加賀海岸国定公園]]に含まれている[https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html 福井県 越前加賀海岸国定公園]。 == 概要 == 面積は約10.2[[ヘクタール]]で、周囲2キロメートルは海食崖になっている{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=「雄島」の地質|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。島全体が[[流紋岩]]でできており、近隣の[[東尋坊]]同様に[[柱状節理]]が発達しているほか、板状節理も見ることができる{{Cite web|和書|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) 雄島の流紋岩（坂井市三国町）|work=福井のニュース ジオっちゃお|publisher=福井新聞|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=ふくいミュージアムNo.19|accessdate=2022-4-20|publisher=福井県立歴史博物館|page=6}}。[[スダジイ]]、[[タブノキ]]、[[シロダモ]]のように温暖な気候を好む植物が生えている{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=「雄島」の植生|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。 島の西側斜面上に[[雄島灯台]]が建てられている。 == 名所 == ; 磁石岩 : 何らかの理由で熱残留磁気が特に強く、近くに置いた[[方位磁針]]の向きがずれるほどの岩が何か所かある。[[雷]]に打たれたせいで磁界ができたとも言われる。 ; 瓜割の水 : 海岸に近い場所に湧く小さな[[泉]]だが、塩気のない真水である。雄島に降った[[雨]]が地中の節理に沿って流れ出た[[地下水]]で、夏でも冷たく、泉に入れた[[ウリ|瓜]]が割れたとされる。 ; 大湊神社 : [[白雉]]年間に勤請されたと伝えられる[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=「大湊神社」の由緒|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。[[源義経]]が落ち延びる際に立ち寄って家臣の[[兜]]を奉納した、社が[[朝倉義景]]の後援を受けていた際に[[明智光秀]]が訪れて[[漢詩]]を詠んだ{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=「雄島」の源（明智）光秀 詩碑|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}という話もある。 : かつての社殿は[[織田信長]]の兵によって焼き払われており、[[21世紀]]に残る社は棟札によると[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）に造立されたものである{{Cite web|和書|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/03.html|title=「大湊神社」の社殿|work=Web大湊神社 安島|publisher=大湊神社|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。本殿・拝殿ともに福井県の文化財に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|date=|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=坂井市|work=福井の文化財|publisher=福井県教育庁生涯学習・文化財課|accessdate=2012-06-03}}。 == ギャラリー == 画像:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|[[東尋坊]]から望む雄島。 File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|磁石岩。先端部の数か所で方位磁針が異常を示す。 File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|瓜割の水 File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|大湊神社 拝殿。この真後ろに本殿がある。 File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|雄島へ渡る雄島橋 File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|雄島から東尋坊を望む File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|大湊神社参道の石段 File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|三国の豪商内田惣右衛門が寄進した石灯篭 File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|タブノキの巨木 File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|ヤブラン File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|キツネノカミソリ File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|クズ File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|センニンソウ == 交通アクセス == * [[北陸新幹線]]・[[ハピラインふくい線]] [[芦原温泉駅]]から [[京福バス]] 85・97系統（東尋坊線）「[[坂井市龍翔博物館|龍翔博物館]]前」行きで「安島（あんとう）」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は[[えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線]] [[あわら湯のまち駅]]からも利用できる。 * えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線 [[三国駅 (福井県)|三国駅]]から 京福バス 84・96系統（東尋坊線）「芦原温泉駅」行きで「安島」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は同線の[[三国港駅]]からも利用できる。 島へは安島漁港から雄島橋が架かっており、徒歩または[[自転車]]でアクセス可能。橋の本土側そばに[[駐車場]]がある。駐車場までは{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[北陸自動車道]] [[金津インターチェンジ|金津IC]]から、主に広域農道「坂井丘陵フルーツライン」経由、約17 km。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ 雄島]（福井県坂井市公式観光ガイド さかい旅ナビ） * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしま}} [[Category:福井県の島]] [[Category:坂井市の地理]]

Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|[[東尋坊]]から望む雄島。 File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|磁石岩。先端部の数か所で方位磁針が異常を示す。 File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|瓜割の水 File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|大湊神社 拝殿。この真後ろに本殿がある。 File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|雄島へ渡る雄島橋 File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|雄島から東尋坊を望む File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|大湊神社参道の石段 File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|三国の豪商内田惣右衛門が寄進した石灯篭 File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|タブノキの巨木 File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|ヤブラン File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|キツネノカミソリ File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|クズ File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|センニンソウ == 交通アクセス == * [[北陸新幹線]]・[[ハピラインふくい線]] [[芦原温泉駅]]から [[京福バス]] 85・97系統（東尋坊線）「[[坂井市龍翔博物館|龍翔博物館]]前」行きで「安島（あんとう）」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は[[えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線]] [[あわら湯のまち駅]]からも利用できる。 * えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線 [[三国駅 (福井県)|三国駅]]から 京福バス 84・96系統（東尋坊線）「芦原温泉駅」行きで「安島」下車、徒歩10分。このバス路線は同線の[[三国港駅]]からも利用できる。 島へは安島漁港から雄島橋が架かっており、徒歩または[[自転車]]でアクセス可能。橋の本土側そばに[[駐車場]]がある。駐車場までは{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[北陸自動車道]] [[金津インターチェンジ|金津IC]]から、主に広域農道「坂井丘陵フルーツライン」経由、約17 km。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ 雄島]（福井県坂井市公式観光ガイド さかい旅ナビ） * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしま}} [[Category:福井県の島]] [[Category:坂井市の地理]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = {{ill|Comparison of area|ja|面積の比較|0.102|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of {{ill|Mikuni, Fukui|ja|三国町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōshima}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]]

{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = {{ill|Comparison of area|ja|面積の比較|0.102|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of {{ill|Mikuni, Fukui|ja|三国町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōshima]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]]

It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the {{ill|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|ja|越前加賀海岸国定公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of {{ill|Rhyolite|ja|流紋岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby {{ill|Tōjinbō|ja|東尋坊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and believed to have been enshrined during the {{ill|Hakuchi Era|ja|白雉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by {{ill|Asakura Yoshikage|ja|朝倉義景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|ja|明智光秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōshima]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]]

Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|スダジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis|qq=}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|ja|タブノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus thunbergii|qq=}}, and {{ill|Neolitsea sericea|ja|シロダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neolitsea sericea|qq=}} grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate.
