File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the {{ill|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|ja|えちぜん鉄道三国芦原線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line|qq=}}. * From {{ill|Mikuni Station (Fukui)|ja|三国駅 (福井県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Station (Fukui)|qq=}} on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to {{ill|Mikuniminato Station|ja|三国港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuniminato Station|qq=}}. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]]

File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:坂井市の地理]] [[Category:福井県の島]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Geography of Sakai, Fukui
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] [[Category:福井県の島]]

File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] [[Category:Islands of Fukui prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] [[Category:Islands of Fukui prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659209]] Wikidata name: Oshima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[:en:Island|Island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}}

It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=|1=Oshima Lighthouse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659211}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{wikidata link|Q11659209}}

Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=|1=Oshima Lighthouse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659211}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659209]] Wikidata name: Oshima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|Oshima in Fukui Prefecture|An island in [[Maizuru, Kyoto Prefecture|Maizuru]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]|Kanmuri Island|An island in Akita Prefecture|{{ill|Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|ja|雄島 (秋田県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Akita Prefecture)|qq=|1=Oshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62016671}}|An island in Miyagi Prefecture|{{ill|Matsushima (Miyagi)|ja|松島#文芸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima (Miyagi)|qq=|1=Matsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1144855}}}} {{Infobox 島 | 島名 = Oshima (雄島) | 画像 = [[File:Oshima_from_above.jpg|300px]]
Aerial view of Oshima | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 15 | 緯度秒 = 6.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 7 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 面積 = [[:en:Orders of magnitude (area)|Comparison of area]] | 周囲 = 2 | 標高 = 27 | 最高峰 = | 最大都市 = | 海域 = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | 国 = {{JPN}} ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|福井県]]) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Oshima}} | OSMズーム = 13 }} '''Oshima''' (雄島) is an uninhabited [[:en:Island|Island]] located in the Anjima area of [[:en:Mikuni, Fukui|Mikuni, Fukui]] in [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai City]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the islands along the [[Echizen Coast]] and the largest among them. The island is included in the [[:en:Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park]].[http://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/doc/shizen/kouen/etizen.html Echizen-Kaga Kaigan Quasi-National Park - Fukui Prefecture] == Overview == The island covers an area of approximately 10.2 hectares and features a 2-kilometer coastline of marine erosion cliffs.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/02.html|title=Geology of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The entire island is composed of [[:en:Rhyolite|Rhyolite]], with well-developed columnar joints similar to those found at nearby [[:en:Tōjinbō|Tōjinbō]]. Plate joints are also visible.{{Cite web|date=2010-12-20|url=http://www.fukuishimbun.co.jp/localnews/niegeo/25419.html|title=(8) Rhyolite of Oshima (Mikuni, Sakai City)|work=Fukui Newspaper - Geology Section|publisher=Fukui Shimbun|accessdate=2012-06-03}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.fukui.lg.jp/muse/Cul-Hist/info/kouhoushi/fm19.pdf|title=Fukui Museum No.19|publisher=Fukui Prefectural Museum of History|page=6|accessdate=2022-04-20}} Warm-climate plants such as [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]], [[:en:Machilus thunbergii|Machilus thunbergii]], and [[:en:Neolitsea sericea|Neolitsea sericea]] grow on the island.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/03.html|title=Vegetation of "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The {{ill|Oshima Lighthouse|ja|雄島灯台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima Lighthouse|qq=|1=Oshima Lighthouse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659211}} is located on the western slope of the island. == Attractions == ; Magnetic Rocks : Some rocks on the island have a particularly strong remanent magnetization, causing compasses nearby to deviate. This is thought to have been caused by lightning strikes. ; Uruwari Spring : A small spring near the coast produces fresh water without any salt content. It is believed to be groundwater from rain that flowed along the island’s subsurface joints. The spring remains cold even in summer, and legend has it that watermelons placed in it would split open. ; Ominato Shrine : An ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and believed to have been enshrined during the [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi Era]].{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/s_1/02.html|title=History of Ominato Shrine|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} The shrine is associated with legends of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], who is said to have stopped there and dedicated a retainer’s helmet. During the shrine's period of patronage by [[:en:Asakura Yoshikage|Asakura Yoshikage]], [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] reportedly visited and composed Chinese poetry.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://www.net24.ne.jp/~oominato/01/o/05.html|title=Poetry Monument of Akechi Mitsuhide on "Oshima"|work=Web Ominato Shrine Anjima|publisher=Ominato Shrine|accessdate=2012-06-03}} : The shrine's original structures were burned by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]’s troops. The existing buildings, dated to 1621 according to a ridge tag, are designated cultural properties of Fukui Prefecture.{{Cite web|date=2001|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/sakai/itiran.html|title=Sakai City Cultural Assets|work=Cultural Assets of Fukui|publisher=Fukui Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2012-06-03}} == Gallery == File:Japan Tojinbo14bs3200.jpg|Oshima viewed from Tōjinbō. File:Magnetic Rocks of Oshima.JPG|Magnetic Rocks. Several areas show compass anomalies. File:Uriwari-no-Mizu Fountain of Oshima.JPG|Uruwari Spring. File:Haiden of Ominato Shrine of Oshima.JPG|Haiden of Ominato Shrine with the honden behind. File:Oshima Bridge in Fukui.jpg|Oshima Bridge connecting to the island. File:A view of Tojin-bo from Oshima.jpg|Tōjinbō as seen from Oshima. File:Stone steps of sando(an approach) of Ominato-jinja in Oshima.jpg|Stone steps leading to Ominato Shrine. File:Kasuga type lantern which were donated by Soemon Uchida.jpg|Kasuga-style stone lantern donated by merchant Soemon Uchida. File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{wikidata link|Q11659209}}

File:Tabunoki (Machilus thunbergii).jpg|A giant Machilus thunbergii tree. File:Yabuta (Liriope muscari).jpg|Liriope muscari. File:Kitsuneno-Kamisori (Lycoris sanguinea).jpg|Lycoris sanguinea (Kitsune no Kamisori). File:Kuzu (Pueraria Montana var. lobata).jpg|Kudzu vine. File:Senninso(Clematis terniflora) in Oshima.jpg|Clematis terniflora. == Transportation Access == * From [[:en:Awaraonsen Station|Awaraonsen Station]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Shinkansen|Hokuriku Shinkansen]] and [[Happiline Fukui Line]], take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 85 or 97 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Ryushokan-mae" and get off at "Antō," followed by a 10-minute walk. This bus route is also accessible from [[:en:Awara-Yunomachi Station|Awara-Yunomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Mikuni Awara Line|Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line]]. * From [[:en:Mikuni Station (Fukui)|Mikuni Station (Fukui)]] on the Echizen Railway Mikuni Awara Line, take the Keifuku Bus Lines No. 84 or 96 (Tojinbo Line) bound for "Awaraonsen Station," get off at "Antō," and walk for 10 minutes. The route also connects to [[:en:Mikuni-Minato Station|Mikuniminato Station]]. Oshima is connected to the mainland by the Oshima Bridge at Anjima Fishing Port. Visitors can cross on foot or by bicycle. A parking lot is available near the bridge on the mainland side. By car, it is approximately 17 km from {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] Kanazu IC via the Sakai Hill Fruit Line. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kanko-sakai.com/spot/k006/ Oshima] (Sakai City Official Travel Guide) * {{commonscat-inline|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{translated page|ja|雄島|version=104322008|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ceb:O-shima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 36,25, long 136,12)]] [[sn:Ōshima]] [[ja:雄島]] [[ja:Ōshima]] [[Category:Geography of Sakai, Fukui]] {{Commons category|Oshima (Sakai, Fukui)}} {{wikidata link|Q11659209}}
